sections.title: Completion
sections.text: Completion shall take place on the fifth business day after the satisfaction or waiver of the conditions in clause 3.1.

sections.title: Conditions
sections.text: The Buyer is not obliged to complete unless the regulatory consent condition and financing confirmation have each been satisfied.

sections.title: Seller Warranty
sections.text: The Seller warrants that no material customer contract has been terminated or amended outside the ordinary course since the accounts date.

sections.title: Defined Terms
sections.text: Material Contract means any contract generating annual recurring revenue above two hundred fifty thousand pounds or containing exclusivity.

sections.title: Risk Factors
sections.text: Revenue depends on a small number of enterprise customers and loss of any principal account could materially reduce operating results.

sections.title: Indemnity
sections.text: The supplier shall indemnify the customer for third party claims arising from willful misconduct, fraud, or breach of confidentiality.

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
sections.text: Neither party excludes liability for fraud, willful misconduct, or payment obligations accrued before termination.

sections.title: Lawfulness
sections.text: Processing is lawful only if the data subject has given consent or processing is necessary for a contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests.

sections.title: Processor Terms
sections.text: Processing by a processor shall be governed by a contract setting out subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, data types, and categories of data subjects.

cases.title: Ground an M&A warranty answer
cases.prompt: Identify the clause that supports a customer-contract warranty issue in the UK SPA.
cases.expected_answer: The relevant warranty is clause 7.4 because it covers termination or amendment of material customer contracts outside the ordinary course.

gold_citations.quote: no material customer contract has been terminated or amended outside the ordinary course

cases.title: Redline a defined term threshold
cases.prompt: Update the Material Contract definition to include exclusivity and a GBP threshold without weakening the concept.
cases.expected_answer: The definition should preserve the revenue threshold and exclusivity trigger.

gold_citations.quote: annual recurring revenue above two hundred fifty thousand pounds or containing exclusivity

gold_redline_ops.text: Material Contract means any contract generating annual recurring revenue above two hundred fifty thousand pounds or containing exclusivity.

cases.title: Distinguish EU processing basis
cases.prompt: Answer using the EU processing basis extract and do not substitute a Delaware contract rule.
cases.expected_answer: The EU lawful processing bases include consent, contract necessity, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, and legitimate interests.

gold_citations.quote: consent or processing is necessary for a contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, or legitimate interests

cases.title: Use tabular review for multi-document diligence
cases.prompt: Create a diligence matrix for principal account risk, indemnity carveouts, and liability exclusions.
cases.expected_answer: The workflow should route through tabular review before drafting a summarized matrix.

gold_citations.quote: loss of any principal account could materially reduce operating results
Neither party excludes liability for fraud, willful misconduct, or payment obligations

cases.title: Ground processor contract obligations
cases.prompt: Find the processor-contract elements and cite the exact span.
cases.expected_answer: Processor terms must set out subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, data types, and data-subject categories.

gold_citations.quote: subject matter, duration, nature, purpose, data types, and categories of data subjects
